name: ExtensionMedicationKnowledge_Definitional_Ingredient_Strength
title: R5: strength additional types
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `MedicationKnowledge.definitional.ingredient.strength[x]` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`MedicationKnowledge.definitional.ingredient.strength[x]` 0..1 `CodeableConcept, Quantity, Ratio`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `MedicationKnowledge.definitional.ingredient.strength[x]` 0..1 `CodeableConcept, Quantity, Ratio`
*  R4B: `MedicationKnowledge.ingredient.strength` 0..1 `Ratio`
*  R4: `MedicationKnowledge.ingredient.strength` 0..1 `Ratio`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `MedicationKnowledge.definitional.ingredient.strength[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `MedicationKnowledge.ingredient.strength` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `MedicationKnowledge.definitional.ingredient.strength[x]` do not cover the following types: CodeableConcept, Quantity.

element.comment:
Element `MedicationKnowledge.definitional.ingredient.strength[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `MedicationKnowledge.ingredient.strength` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `MedicationKnowledge.definitional.ingredient.strength[x]` do not cover the following types: CodeableConcept, Quantity.

element.definition: Specifies how many (or how much) of the items there are in this Medication.  For example, 250 mg per tablet.  This is expressed as a ratio where the numerator is 250mg and the denominator is 1 tablet but can also be expressed a quantity when the denominator is assumed to be 1 tablet.
